stories.headline: Prosecution prepares 10,000 exhibits for Sara Duterte impeachment trial as July 6 start approaches
stories.summary: The House prosecution panel is preparing around 10,000 pieces of evidence for Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, which the Senate Impeachment Court projects will run seven to eight months starting July 6. Prosecutors may waive certain articles if early evidence proves compelling, and they are seeking up to 63 hearing dates compared to the defense's 30. The four charges include alleged misuse of confidential funds, unexplained wealth, assassination threats against President Marcos and his family, and pressuring officials to bypass procurement rules.
stories.context: A Senate impeachment court pre-trial order setting the framework for facts, witnesses, and trial dates is due June 29. Duterte faces charges covering alleged misuse of confidential funds, false financial disclosures, unexplained wealth, and alleged threats against President Marcos and his family.
stories.watch_for: The pre-trial order is due June 29; the trial proper opens July 6, with hearings initially scheduled on Mondays to Wednesdays.

stories.headline: NBI files human trafficking charges against former congressman Defensor and family members
stories.summary: The National Bureau of Investigation filed qualified human trafficking charges against former Anakalusugan Representative Mike Defensor, his wife Julie, daughter Michaela, and son Miguel Gabriel, alleging they owned establishments in Pasig City where more than 50 women were sexually exploited. The case stems from an April 21 raid on Chicago KTV Bar and Bleu Hotel in Pasig City. Defensor denied the charges as politically motivated; NBI Director Melvin Matibag said the evidence against him was strong.

stories.headline: Government submits proposed P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027
stories.summary: The Department of Budget and Management submitted to Congress a proposed P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027, about P407 billion or 6 percent higher than the 2026 outlay and equal to roughly 21.7 percent of GDP. The framework, signed by acting Secretary Kim Robert de Leon, cited narrow fiscal space due to mandatory spending and projected interest payments of about P950 billion.
stories.watch_for: Congress convenes its second regular session on July 24, when deliberations on the 2027 budget are expected to begin.

stories.headline: Defense department and Chinese embassy trade accusations over South China Sea ahead of arbitral ruling anniversary
stories.summary: The Department of National Defense accused the Chinese Embassy in Manila of insincerity and duplicity after the embassy again called the 2016 arbitration award on the South China Sea illegal and void. The Department of Foreign Affairs reaffirmed the ruling as final and binding and part of international law. The exchange comes as both sides escalate rhetoric over the West Philippine Sea.
stories.context: The 2016 Permanent Court of Arbitration ruling rejected China's expansive nine-dash-line claim. China has never recognized the award.
stories.watch_for: The ruling marks its 10th anniversary on July 12, 2026.

stories.headline: New US ambassador Lee Lipton arrives in Manila
stories.summary: The US Embassy said new US Ambassador to the Philippines Lee Lipton arrived in Manila on June 28 to begin his posting, replacing MaryKay Carlson. Lipton, a Florida businessman appointed by President Trump in October 2025, has no prior career diplomatic experience. The embassy said his priorities include security cooperation, supply chains, trade, and the Luzon Economic Corridor initiative.
stories.context: The arrival comes amid heightened Philippines-China tensions in the West Philippine Sea and continuing US-Philippine defense and economic engagement.

stories.headline: Senate committee to examine Juvenile Justice Act after violent incidents involving minors
stories.summary: A Senate committee led by Sen. Erwin Tulfo said it will conduct a comprehensive review of how the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act is being implemented, prompted by recent incidents involving minors including a shooting at a Tacloban school and stabbings in Negros Occidental and Cavite. The review will examine rehabilitation facilities, parental accountability, intervention programs, and whether the current age of criminal liability remains appropriate. Sen. Robin Padilla proposed lowering the minimum age to 10 years old, drawing criticism from child welfare advocates.
stories.context: Lowering the minimum age of criminal responsibility has been debated in the Philippines for years. The panel said it will gather input from legal experts, law enforcement, social workers, and affected families.

stories.headline: Senate names anti-dynasty bill and social media age restriction among LEDAC legislative priorities
stories.summary: Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian announced that an anti-political dynasty bill, a social media restriction for users aged 16 and under, and amendments to the party-list system are among 50 priority measures endorsed by the Legislative-Executive Development Advisory Council. Gatchalian said the social media measure would require age verification through facial recognition and give the Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center expanded blocking powers.
stories.context: The social media proposal gained renewed urgency after a fatal school shooting in Tacloban City. Gatchalian noted that only five new Bahay Pag-asa youth rehabilitation centers are under construction nationwide despite 84 provinces needing coverage.
stories.watch_for: Bicameral conference deliberations on the anti-dynasty bill, where significant differences between Senate and House versions are expected.

stories.headline: General Santos suspends in-person classes after magnitude 6.5 earthquake off Davao Occidental
stories.summary: General Santos City officials suspended in-person classes at public schools pending structural assessments after a magnitude 6.5 earthquake centered about 70 kilometers west of Balut Island in Davao Occidental was felt at Intensity V in parts of Mindanao. Schools shifted to alternative delivery modes while buildings are inspected. PHIVOLCS said the tremor was a separate event and not an aftershock of the magnitude 7.8 quake that struck the region earlier in June.
stories.context: Tremors were also felt in Sarangani, South Cotabato, and Sultan Kudarat provinces. The June 8 magnitude 7.8 quake struck the same Mindanao region.
stories.watch_for: Results of structural assessments of school buildings before classes resume.

stories.headline: Two workers trapped after road works site collapse in Las Pinas; rescue operations underway
stories.summary: Two workers remained missing Sunday evening after a structural collapse at a road works site connected to a water supply project in Pamplona II, Las Pinas City. Five other people at the site were rescued, one with injuries. The Bureau of Fire Protection, Philippine National Police, and the city disaster risk reduction office conducted retrieval operations.

stories.headline: PAGASA says low-pressure area and monsoon to bring rains over parts of the country
stories.summary: In its 4 p.m. bulletin on June 28, PAGASA said a low-pressure area about 705 kilometers east of Surigao City and an active southwest monsoon would bring moderate to at times heavy rains over several areas. The agency flagged the risk of flash floods and landslides in Eastern Visayas, Caraga, the Davao region, Catanduanes, Albay, and Sorsogon, and monsoon rains over Ilocos, Zambales, Bataan, Occidental Mindoro, and Palawan.
stories.context: PAGASA said no tropical cyclone was inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility as of June 28; the hazard is rainfall from the low-pressure area and the seasonal southwest monsoon.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA reissues its general weather forecast roughly every 12 hours and would begin numbered tropical cyclone bulletins if the low-pressure area develops into a tropical depression.

stories.headline: Experts warn pollutants from monthlong Navotas landfill fire could persist for years
stories.summary: Environmental experts warned that pollutants released by a 30-day fire at the Navotas landfill, which began April 10 and burned more than half of its 41-hectare area, could linger in Metro Manila's environment for years and pose long-term health risks. They identified dioxins and furans among the most hazardous pollutants, with possible effects ranging from respiratory irritation to cardiovascular disease and cancer, and urged expanded air-quality and gas monitoring.
stories.context: The fire, which experts said was likely triggered by methane buildup, was extinguished after about 30 days.

stories.headline: Police say White Ribbon March at EDSA was peaceful and orderly
stories.summary: The Philippine National Police said the White Ribbon March, an interfaith anti-corruption demonstration, was peaceful and orderly on June 28. The PNP said about 3,600 participants gathered at the People Power Monument along EDSA in Quezon City after a Mass and march from the EDSA Shrine, with nearly 10,000 officers deployed for security and traffic management. Organizers called for accountability and action against corruption and political dynasties.
stories.context: The march was backed by religious and civil society groups, including the Interreligious Leaders Council for National Transformation.

stories.headline: Philippines advances investment for Southeast Asia's first offshore wind project in Camarines Sur
stories.summary: Finance Secretary Frederick Go met with executives of Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners and ACEN Corporation to advance a 901-megawatt offshore wind project in San Miguel Bay, Camarines Sur. The government says the project would be Southeast Asia's first offshore wind farm, aimed at strengthening long-term energy security, creating jobs, and aligning with the 2023-2050 Philippine Energy Plan.

stories.headline: WTO review flags high Philippine trade costs as a hurdle to investment
stories.summary: In its sixth Trade Policy Review of the Philippines, the World Trade Organization said high trade costs remain a key obstacle, estimating logistics costs at about 27 percent of retail prices and overall trade costs at roughly 20 percent above the ASEAN average. The WTO urged the country to ease regulatory uncertainty and close infrastructure gaps to attract investors and deepen its role in electronics and semiconductor supply chains, while crediting ongoing digitalization and liberalization reforms.
stories.context: Trade Policy Reviews are periodic WTO assessments of a member's trade regime.

stories.headline: Trade department to recommend lifting 2019 freeze on new Metro Manila economic zones
stories.summary: The Department of Trade and Industry said it will recommend to Malacanang the lifting of Administrative Order No. 18, the 2019 moratorium that halted new PEZA-registered economic zones in Metro Manila to steer development to the provinces. Trade Secretary Ma. Cristina Roque said the department will endorse lifting the order, and PEZA Director-General Tereso Panga said his agency has been cleared to formally endorse it to the Office of the President.
stories.context: PEZA accreditation confers tax and customs incentives sought by IT-BPO and locator firms. Several office buildings in Makati and Taguig are awaiting accreditation.
stories.watch_for: The recommendation now awaits approval by the Office of the President.

stories.headline: Oil industry source projects fuel price increases next week amid Middle East tensions
stories.summary: An oil industry source told GMA News that pump prices could rise next week by up to P2.00 per liter for gasoline and P1.00 per liter for diesel, citing renewed tensions between the United States and Iran. The projected increases would reverse rollbacks implemented days earlier. The figures are estimates and not yet final.
stories.watch_for: Oil companies typically announce final weekly price adjustments on Tuesdays.

stories.headline: Quezon City condo residents contest Maynilad's reclassification to semi-business water rates
stories.summary: About 1,824 units at Smile Citihomes Condominium in Barangay Kaligayahan, Quezon City received a disconnection notice for approximately P1.3 million in disputed bills after Maynilad reclassified the building from residential to semi-business water rates in March 2026, citing fewer than 10 small commercial tenants. The condominium board obtained a temporary hold order from the Metropolitan Waterworks and Sewerage System Regulatory Office suspending the disconnection while the dispute is reviewed.
stories.watch_for: Decision from the MWSS Regulatory Office on the building's water rate classification.

stories.headline: Four wounded in shootout between police and armed group in Arayat, Pampanga
stories.summary: Police said four people were wounded in a shootout in Arayat, Pampanga, after residents reported indiscriminate gunfire and the armed group opened fire on responding officers rather than surrender. Police said one of the wounded was a former communist rebel who surrendered in 2018 and headed a local armed group. Authorities said they recovered several firearms and a grenade, and all four wounded were hospitalized.

stories.headline: Senator Hontiveros opens 2028 presidential campaign with Bacolod rally drawing 3,000 supporters
stories.summary: Senator Risa Hontiveros formally launched her 2028 presidential campaign in Bacolod City on June 28, with the 'Risa Na' movement drawing about 3,000 supporters to Gonzaga Gym in Barangay Taculing. Attendees signed a manifesto pledging to organize community chapters, and backers presented Hontiveros as a candidate without dynastic political ties.

stories.headline: PDEA reports P40 million in drugs seized and 110 arrested in week of operations
stories.summary: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency said it conducted 83 anti-drug operations nationwide from June 18 to 25, seizing about P40.06 million worth of illegal drugs and arresting 110 suspects. The agency said seizures included roughly 3,575 grams of shabu and more than 72,000 marijuana plants, and that 76 of those arrested were tagged as pushers and 19 as possessors.

stories.headline: Philippines leads ASEAN adoption of three higher education initiatives covering digital access and research
stories.summary: The Commission on Higher Education secured approval from ASEAN member states for three regional education initiatives at a June 26 ministers' roundtable hosted by the Philippines. The approved measures establish a framework for digital transformation and sustainability in higher education, create a regional teacher development center called ACTED, and expand the ASEAN GEMS Plus scholarship and researcher mobility program. The initiatives will guide the ASEAN Work Plan on Education 2026-2030.

stories.headline: Cagayan's Rina Andrea Delos Santos crowned Miss Philippines Earth 2026
stories.summary: Rina Andrea Delos Santos of Ballesteros, Cagayan, was crowned Miss Philippines Earth 2026 at the pageant's coronation night in Bukidnon on June 27, competing against 31 other candidates under the theme Love in Action. She also won the Miss Hana special award. The pageant named four Elemental Queens during the ceremony.
stories.context: The Miss Philippines Earth winner represents the country in the international Miss Earth pageant.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon two days after US-brokered ceasefire framework is signed
stories.summary: Israeli aircraft conducted strikes in southern Lebanon on June 28, just two days after a US-brokered framework was signed on June 26 in Washington to end a conflict that has killed more than 4,200 people in Lebanon since March 2026. One Lebanese civilian was killed in the strikes -- the first death since the agreement. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem called the deal humiliating, while far-right Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir also opposed it.
stories.context: The agreement's core ambiguity -- whether Israel must withdraw unconditionally or can remain until Hezbollah disarms -- has generated conflicting claims from all parties. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio described the deal as the beginning of the beginning, while Israeli PM Netanyahu called it historic.
stories.watch_for: Implementation of ceasefire withdrawal and disarmament provisions, and Iran's response to continued Israeli military operations in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange new strikes as Iran targets US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain
stories.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it launched missiles and drones at US installations in Kuwait and Bahrain on June 28, a day after US strikes on Iranian targets near the Strait of Hormuz that Washington said followed a drone attack on a commercial tanker. Both sides accused each other of violating a June interim agreement. Iran's Guards warned the escalation could halt diplomatic efforts to end the conflict.
stories.context: The conflict began with strikes on Iran in February 2026. The Strait of Hormuz carries a large share of seaborne oil, and Asian economies including Japan and South Korea source much of their crude through it.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,400 as rescuers race to find survivors
stories.summary: Twin earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 that struck Venezuela on June 25 have killed at least 1,430 people, with tens of thousands reported missing, devastating the coastal La Guaira area near Caracas. With a shortage of heavy equipment, residents dug through rubble by hand as international rescuers arrived; officials said about 1,600 foreign rescue personnel had reached the country as the critical 72-hour survival window closed.
stories.context: The mainshock is described as Venezuela's strongest in over a century. Caracas's main airport was heavily damaged, and the UN warned that millions may need shelter, water, and medical care.

stories.headline: European heatwave kills over 1,000 in France as temperature records fall across the continent
stories.summary: France's public health agency reported at least 1,000 excess deaths from a severe heatwave, with most fatalities among older people. At least 191 million Europeans faced temperatures of 35 degrees Celsius or higher, and Germany, Denmark, and the Czech Republic set preliminary all-time temperature records, with some German locations exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Scientists attributed the intensity to an omega block weather pattern made virtually impossible without man-made climate change.

stories.headline: Ukraine says it struck two Russian oil refineries hundreds of kilometers from front lines
stories.summary: Ukraine conducted overnight drone strikes on the Slavyansk refinery in Krasnodar region and the Yaroslavl refinery approximately 700 kilometers from the front line. Russian authorities reported at least two deaths and said air defenses shot down 117 drones across multiple regions. President Zelenskyy said the attacks were aimed at weakening Russia's ability to sustain its military campaign.
